source_docs.title: Droplet Barcoding for Single-Cell Transcriptomics Applied to Embryonic Stem Cells

oligos.name: Hydrogel-incorporated (acrydite) common DNA primer
oligos.role: Bead-anchored common primer (T7 promoter + PE1/Read1 stub) polymerized into the hydrogel; photo-releasable
oligos.notes: 5' modifications /5Acryd/ (acrydite, couples into polyacrylamide mesh) and /iSpPC/ (internal photocleavable spacer, UV-released at 365 nm). Contains 5' handle CGATGACG, T7 RNA-pol promoter TAATACGACTCACTATAGGG, a ATACCACCATGG segment, and the PE1/Read1 sequence CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTC. Serves as the starting 5'-Ac-PC-T7p-PE1 primer for split-pool barcoding.

oligos.name: Round-1 split-pool oligo (W1*-bc1-PE1*)
oligos.role: 384-well primer that extends the bead primer to add the first-half cell barcode (bc1) via Bst; templates PE1* and W1*
oligos.notes: 5'-W1*(AAGGCGTCACAAGCAATCACTC) - bc1 (unique per well; inDrop v1 bc1 is variable length ~8-11 bp to preserve base balance) - PE1*(AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAG)-3'. * = reverse complement. One of 384 sequences.

oligos.name: Round-2 split-pool oligo (T19V*-UMI-bc2-W1*)
oligos.role: 384-well primer that adds the second-half cell barcode (bc2), the random hexamer UMI and the poly-T capture tail
oligos.notes: 5'-T19V*(B + A19; B = non-A anchor base, complement of the 3' V of the poly-T) - UMI (NNNNNN, random hexamer) - bc2 (8 nt, unique per well) - W1*(AAGGCGTCACAAGCAATCACTC)-3'. One of 384 sequences.

oligos.name: Fully assembled barcoded RT/capture primer (released from BHM)
oligos.role: Photo-released reverse-transcription primer; poly-T primes mRNA poly-A and tags cDNA with cell barcode + UMI; carries T7 promoter for later IVT

components.name: T7 promoter
components.role: T7 RNA polymerase promoter for in vitro transcription / linear amplification

components.name: PE1 / Read1 primer site
components.role: Illumina Read1 / PE1 priming sequence (ends in CGATCT)

components.name: W1 adaptor
components.role: constant spacer separating the two barcode halves; used for read filtering

components.name: poly-T
components.role: poly-T(19)+V anchored capture of mRNA poly-A

oligos.notes: Assembled by two split-pool Bst extensions on the bead common primer. 147,456 (384^2) possible barcodes. This is the molecule released by UV cleavage of /iSpPC/ that primes in-drop RT.

oligos.name: 3' RNA ligation adapter (post-IVT)
oligos.role: Pre-adenylated/phosphorylated adapter ligated to 3' end of fragmented amplified antisense RNA; provides the PE2/Read2 side priming site
oligos.notes: 5' /5Phos/, 3' /3SpC3/ blocked. From the Jaitin CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq library workflow (ref. 2).

oligos.name: Second RT primer (2nd RT)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcribes the adapter-ligated amplified RNA into cDNA for the enrichment PCR
oligos.notes: Anneals to the ligated adapter region (GGCATTCCTGCTGAAC = revcomp of the adapter's GTTCAGCAGGAATGCC); adds a GTCTC 5' tail that becomes the PE2 priming site.

oligos.name: PCR enrichment primer P5 (PE1 side)
oligos.role: Library enrichment PCR forward primer; adds Illumina P5 and completes the Read1/PE1 sequence
oligos.notes: P5 (AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC) + PE1 3' portion (TCTTTCCCTACACGA). Flipped P5/PE1 orientation vs. the original CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq protocol.

oligos.name: PCR enrichment primer P7 (PE2 side)
oligos.role: Library enrichment PCR reverse primer; adds Illumina P7 and the PE2/Read2 handle onto the ligated-adapter side
oligos.notes: P7 (CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT) + PE2 (CGGTCTCGGCATTCCTGCTGAAC). No in-line i7 sample index in the base v1 design; extra library barcodes were added only when splitting large emulsions.

oligos.name: FAM-PE1* FISH probe
oligos.role: 5'-FAM QC probe for the un-extended PE1 stub on beads
oligos.notes: 5' /56-FAM/. Used to quantify primer incorporation on BHMs.

oligos.name: FAM-W1* FISH probe
oligos.role: 5'-FAM QC probe for the W1 sequence after one extension step
oligos.notes: 5' /56-FAM/. Reverse complement of the W1 adaptor GAGTGATTGCTTGTGACGCCTT.

oligos.name: FAM-A20 FISH probe
oligos.role: 5'-FAM QC probe for the poly-T tail after the second extension step
oligos.notes: 5' /56-FAM/ (dA20). High-salt hybridization required due to weak poly-dA binding.

final_library.source_label: inDrop v1 (Klein et al., 2015) — Table S5 assembled primer + CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq enrichment primers
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [P5] TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT [PE1/Read1] [CELL_BARCODE:8] (bc1) GAGTGATTGCTTGTGACGCCTT [W1] [CELL_BARCODE:8] (bc2) [UMI:6] TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT [polyT] [CDNA] AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACC [ligated adapter + PE2] GATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG [P7] -3'
annotation_lines: GAGTGATTGCTTGTGACGCCTT — W1 adaptor (separates bc1 and bc2)
TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT — poly-T capture tract (from oligo-dT priming of poly-A)
[CDNA] — 3' end of transcript cDNA (Read 2 maps here)
AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACC — ligated 3' RNA adapter + PE2/Read2 handle

evidence.locator: inDrop v1 (Klein et al., 2015) — Table S5 assembled primer + CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq enrichment primers

library_generation.title: Synthesize barcoded hydrogel microspheres (BHMs)
library_generation.summary: Acrydite common primer (T7 promoter + PE1 stub) is co-polymerized into 63 µm polyacrylamide beads, then combinatorially barcoded by two rounds of split-pool Bst extension to build one of 147,456 photo-releasable RT primers per bead.
library_generation.note: Round 1 adds bc1 + W1* (384-well plate); round 2 adds UMI + bc2 + poly-T (384-well plate). Each bead carries ~10^9 identical primers coupled via an internal photocleavable spacer.

library_generation.title: Droplet co-encapsulation and UV primer release
library_generation.summary: Single cell + one BHM + RT/lysis mix are encapsulated in a 4 nL droplet; UV (365 nm, 8 min) cleaves the photocleavable spacer, releasing barcoded primers and lysing the cell to free mRNA.
library_generation.note: Near-100% single-bead occupancy from close packing; cells loaded at ~1 per 5-10 drops to minimize doublets.

library_generation.title: In-drop reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: Poly-T primes the mRNA poly-A and SuperScript III makes first-strand cDNA, tagging each molecule with the cell barcode and UMI.
library_generation.note: 50 °C for 2 h, then 70 °C for 15 min, all within the intact emulsion.

library_generation.title: Break emulsion, pool, and clean up
library_generation.summary: Droplets are demulsified with perfluorooctanol, pooled, and treated with ExoI (+HinfI to cut primer dimers) to remove unused primers.
library_generation.note: Aqueous phase spun to pellet debris/gels; processed per modified CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq protocol.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: The barcoded cDNA is converted to double-stranded DNA, regenerating a functional double-stranded T7 promoter.

library_generation.title: T7 in vitro transcription (linear amplification)
library_generation.summary: T7 RNA polymerase transcribes off the promoter to linearly amplify each molecule into antisense RNA (aRNA) carrying PE1-bc1-W1-bc2-UMI-polyA and the antisense transcript.
library_generation.note: Linear (not exponential) amplification suppresses PCR bias; combined with UMIs this gives the method its low technical noise.

library_generation.title: RNA fragmentation
library_generation.summary: Amplified aRNA is chemically fragmented to sequenceable size.

library_generation.title: 3' adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: A pre-blocked RNA adapter is ligated to the 3' end of each fragment, providing the second (PE2/Read2) priming handle.
library_generation.note: Adapter /5Phos/AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCC/3SpC3/. Samples treated with shrimp alkaline phosphatase before ligation.

library_generation.title: Second reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: Primer GTCTCGGCATTCCTGCTGAAC anneals to the ligated adapter and reverse-transcribes the fragment into cDNA, installing the PE2 sequence.
library_generation.note: Converts the adapter-ligated RNA back to DNA for PCR.
library_generation.product: ds fragment with PE1 on one end and PE2 (from second RT primer) on the ligated-adapter end

library_generation.title: Library enrichment PCR
library_generation.summary: P5 primer (AATGATACGGCGACC...) and P7 primer (CAAGCAGAAGACGG...) amplify and add the Illumina flow-cell adapters, yielding the final sequenceable library (10-13 cycles).
library_generation.note: P5/PE1 and P7/PE2 are in flipped orientation relative to the original CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq protocol.

library_sequencing.note: Read 1 primer anneals to the bottom strand at the PE1/Read1 site and extends rightward, reading the two-part cell barcode separated by the W1 adaptor plus the UMI: bc1 (~8-11 nt) - W1 (22 nt) - bc2 (8 nt) - UMI (6 nt), then runs into the poly-T tract. Reads are retained only if both barcode halves flank the W1 sequence.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT------------------------------------------------------->  Read 1
5'- CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNNNN GAGTGATTGCTTGTGACGCCTT NNNNNNNN NNNNNN TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTV [cDNA] -3'
3'- GAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA NNNNNNNN CTCACTAACGAACACTGCGGAA NNNNNNNN NNNNNN AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAB [cDNA] -5'
       PE1 / Read 1 site            bc1(8-11)      W1 adaptor         bc2(8)   UMI(6)      poly-T(19)V

library_sequencing.primer: PE2 / Read 2 primer (anneals to ligated 3' adapter via 5'-GGCATTCCTGCTGAAC-3')
library_sequencing.note: Read 2 primer anneals to the top-strand ligated-adapter region (GTTCAGCAGGAATGCC) and extends leftward, reading the 3' cDNA fragment from the P7/PE2 side; the read is mapped to the reference transcriptome.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- [cDNA]---AGATCGGAAGAGCGGTTCAGCAGGAATGCCGAGACCGATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- [cDNA]---TCTAGCCTTCTCGCCAAGTCGTCCTTACGGCTCTGGCTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                        3'-CAAGTCGTCCTTACGG-5'   Read 2 primer
    <------------------------- Read 2 (reads cDNA on bottom strand)

title: inDrop (v1)
description: inDrop (indexing droplets) is a droplet-microfluidic single-cell RNA-seq method. Single cells are co-encapsulated with a barcoded hydrogel microsphere (BHM) carrying ~10^9 photo-releasable RT primers encoding one of 147,456 (384×384) combinatorial cell barcodes, plus lysis and reverse-transcription reagents. After UV release, mRNA is captured on the poly-T primer and barcoded during in-drop reverse transcription; pooled cDNA is then linearly amplified by T7 in vitro transcription (CEL-Seq/MARS-Seq workflow) and converted into an Illumina library. Read 1 recovers the two-part cell barcode (separated by the W1 adaptor) plus the UMI; Read 2 is the 3' cDNA that is mapped to the transcriptome.

reference.label: Droplet Barcoding for Single-Cell Transcriptomics Applied to Embryonic Stem Cells

original_publication.title: Droplet Barcoding for Single-Cell Transcriptomics Applied to Embryonic Stem Cells

throughput.summary: ~2,000-3,000 cells barcoded per 100 µL emulsion; cells captured at 4,000-12,000/hour; theoretical capacity of tens of thousands of cells per run. Over 10,000 barcoded cells/controls profiled, ~3,000 sequenced deeply.
throughput.cells: 2,000-3,000 barcoded per 100 µL emulsion (1% multi-barcoding limit at n=2,949 cells for 384^2 barcodes)
throughput.rna: mRNA capture efficiency ~7.1% (from ERCC spike-ins); ~4.5% estimated in cells
throughput.dna: ~10^9 barcoded primers per hydrogel bead; 147,456 (384×384) distinct cell barcodes

publication.statistical_model: Binomial sampling model of mRNA capture (sampling without replacement, efficiency beta); UMI counts modeled with Poisson / Negative Binomial statistics, with above-Poisson noise metric (CV^2 - 1/mean) for biological variability.

other.label: Model system
other.value: Mouse embryonic stem (ES) cells (IB10, 129/Ola) before/after LIF withdrawal; human K562 as species-mixing control

other.label: Companion method
other.value: Published alongside Drop-seq (Macosko et al., 2015, same Cell issue)

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by the two cell-barcode halves flanking the constant W1 adaptor to assign each read to a cell; Read 2 (cDNA) is mapped to the reference transcriptome and reads are collapsed with random UMIs into UMI-filtered mapped (UMIFM) molecule counts, then normalized and analyzed by PCA/t-SNE and correlation-network neighborhood analysis.
data_processing.statistical_model: Binomial sampling model of mRNA capture (efficiency b≈7%); UMI molecule counts modeled with Poisson / Negative Binomial statistics, using an above-Poisson noise metric (CV^2 − 1/mean) to isolate biological variability.
